compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
RISK 1 (cross-session duplication) + client reliability. Two competing prompt-authoring WRITE backends both wired into the editor: `prompt_authoring.save_prompt` (atomic temp-file + `os.replace`) and `prompt_library.create_prompt` (NON-atomic `path.write_text`, `prompt_library.py:240`). They write the same `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` with divergent semantics — one atomic, one a latent half-write on crash; one rejects uppercase ids, the other accepts them.

## Why
`prompt_library` was added as an "additive" layer without folding in the pre-existing atomic writer two directories away. The editor exposes BOTH `/prompt/save` and `/library/create` POST endpoints (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py`), so a client can corrupt a prompt file via the non-atomic path. `create_prompt` is essentially a strict subset of `save_prompt` minus the atomicity + id-rule guarantees.

## What / files
- `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py:236-245` — make `create_prompt`/`_write` DELEGATE to `prompt_authoring.save_prompt` (project-forced target) instead of `path.write_text`.
- Pick ONE id rule (prefer `prompt_authoring._valid_id`); reconcile `PromptExistsError` vs `PromptWriteError` semantics.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` — route `/library/create` through the atomic writer.
Keep the `enumerate_library`/criterion-kind classification that `prompt_library` legitimately adds (only the WRITE path is duplicated, not the enumerate path).

## Scope
In: one atomic write path, one id rule, shared error semantics. Out: the enumerate/classification surface (keep), the prompt schema/front-matter format.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All prompt writes (both editor endpoints) go through the atomic `os.replace` writer — no `write_text` write path remains.
- [ ] A single id-validation rule governs both endpoints (documented).
- [ ] A crash/interrupt mid-write cannot leave a partially-written `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` (test via a write that fails before replace).
- [ ] Existing prompt-authoring + library tests pass; `enumerate_library` behavior unchanged.
compiled_state.title: Risk 1: consolidate the two prompt-authoring write backends
